chapter_summary: Chapter 5 begins with a call to listen carefully to wisdom and think before acting. It ends with an important reminder: God sees everything we do. Nothing is hidden from Him. When we remember that God is always watching — not to catch us doing wrong, but because He loves us — it helps us choose what is right. People who go the wrong way get caught in their own choices, but those who follow God walk in freedom.
main_lesson: God sees everything you do. Choose what is right because God loves you and is always with you.
memory_verse: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
parent_guide: Proverbs 5 addresses sexual faithfulness (vv.3-20), content appropriate for older audiences, which has been omitted in this children's edition. The selected verses focus on the opening call to wisdom (vv.1-2) and the closing theological principle (vv.21-23): God's omniscience and the natural consequences of wrong choices. These themes are rich and accessible for young children. Help your child understand that 'God sees everything' is not a threat — it is a comfort. God knows our struggles, our temptations, and our victories. He cares about all of it.
application_for_children: Practise asking yourself this week: 'Would I do this if God were standing right next to me?' — because He is. When you choose to do right even when no one is watching, you are living wisely.

verses.kjv: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
verses.little_bible: My child, pay close attention to wisdom. Lean in and listen carefully to understanding.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lean in and listen to wisdom.
verses.meaning: Real wisdom requires our full attention — not half-listening, but leaning in with our whole mind and heart.
verses.memory_phrase: I lean in and listen to wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me pay full attention when You or wise people are teaching me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it look like to really, truly listen — not just hear?
verses.family_discussion: How can we become a family that really listens — to God and to each other?
verses.do_it_today: When someone is speaking to you today, put everything else down and look at them. Really listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child leaning forward with wide, attentive eyes and a gentle smile, listening carefully to a wise teacher

verses.kjv: That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
verses.little_bible: Think before you act, and say only what is wise and good.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Think carefully before you speak or act.
verses.meaning: Wisdom means pausing before we act or speak — checking our heart and mind before our words or actions go out.
verses.memory_phrase: I think before I speak or act.
verses.prayer: God, help me pause and think before I speak or do anything today.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you say something without thinking first?
verses.family_discussion: Tell about a time someone's kind, thoughtful words made a big difference.
verses.do_it_today: Before you say something today, ask yourself: 'Is it true? Is it kind? Is it helpful?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing with a finger on their chin, thinking carefully before speaking, with gentle light around them

verses.kjv: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
verses.little_bible: God sees every step you take. He watches over your whole life and knows everything you do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God sees everything you do. He is always watching over you.
verses.meaning: Nothing is hidden from God. He sees every thought, word, and action — and because He loves us, He cares about all of them.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember You are always with me and always watching over me.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that God can see everything — even the secret things?
verses.family_discussion: How is 'God sees everything' a comfort, not just a warning?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good, kind thing today that no one else will see — just you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child doing something kind quietly in a corner, with God's gentle eyes watching warmly from above

verses.kjv: His own iniquities shall take the wicked, and he shall be holden with the cords of his own sin.
verses.little_bible: When people keep doing wrong, they get trapped — like a knot that ties itself tighter and tighter.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Doing wrong things ties us up like a knot. God's way sets us free.
verses.meaning: Sin has a way of trapping us — each wrong choice makes the next one easier and harder to escape. God's way leads to freedom.
verses.prayer: God, when I am tempted to do wrong, help me remember it leads to a trap. Your way leads to freedom.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen how one small wrong choice leads to another, bigger one?
verses.family_discussion: How does following God keep us free instead of trapped?
verses.do_it_today: If there is a bad habit or wrong choice you keep making, tell God about it and ask Him to help you get free.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two paths: one leading to tangled ropes, one leading to wide open green fields with sunlight
